title: Build a coherent single-subject deck set (≥6 decks / ≥1,500 cards)
context: The M2 milestone proves that the pipeline scales to a coherent, curriculum-covering subject set—the depth required for a learner to meaningfully use the decks for study. A single subject with at least 6 decks and 1,500 cards is the minimum to demonstrate curriculum coverage, trigger educator validation, and generate meaningful adoption metrics. All high-stakes cards (if any) require expert sign-off.
objective: Build, accuracy-review, and publish at least 6 decks with at least 1,500 total cards covering one coherent curriculum subject, with zero license/provenance violations and zero known factual errors.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 6 decks with at least 1,500 total cards published and installable in one coherent subject.
Zero license/provenance violations; zero known factual errors after human accuracy review.
All high-stakes cards (medical, legal, financial, safety, exam) expert-signed and carrying 'study aid, not advice' framing; civic content non-partisan.
All cards carry full per-card attribution; all decks build deterministically and update non-destructively.
Reviewer-corrected rate and error-taxonomy mix measured and recorded for the full subject set.
PR includes all deck JSON files, accuracy-review records, and a subject-completion report.
output: A merged PR containing at least 6 CrowdAnki JSON deck files (1,500+ cards) in decks/ covering one coherent subject, published via the full pipeline, with accuracy-review records and a subject-completion report.
